item.title: Fortnite: All Sprite Chest Locations (& Best Route)
item.description:
Sprites are running amok in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3, and each of them has its own unique power and rarity, which is why you might be on a quest to hunt them all and add them to your collection! Luckily for you, we know where they are.

Below, you'll find a full Fortnite map showcasing all Sprite Chest locations , which you can use as a guide while venturing through the map to locate and open any active spawns to see if you get lucky enough to catch a rare Sprite, like the Zero Point, or even a Gold!

Sprite Chests Locations Map in Fortnite

Above, you can find the locations of every potential spawn of a Sprite Chest in Fortnite . It's worth noting that there won't be a Sprite Chest at every one of these spots in a single game, as Sprite Chests will spawn at random at the locations provided , and we may have missed a few considering the map is so large.

For hunting Sprite Chests, I'd recommend making sure you have Visualized Sounds on, as that will provide you with a direction on the HUD when near a Sprite Chest. I'd also recommend trying to hunt them in Zero Build, thanks to the Shockwave Grenade you can get every 30 seconds to navigate the map more easily.

All Sprites can be found in Sprite Chests, and we've got the odds and chances as percentage values for you to see right here.

If you're attempting to hunt Sprites in bot lobbies, it's worth noting that Fortnite added a hotfix where only Water, Fire, and Earth Sprites will appear in this mode.

Best Route to Take to Farm Sprite Chests in Fortnite

The link above will direct you to a guide showcasing our preferred route for finding Sprite Chests in a single match. This guide also covers the path in a video walkthrough (and a map) so you can see exactly where we go so you can avoid hostile players and focus purely on Sprites.

Instead of spending time farming Sprites, you can always try to trade with the community! Click the link provided, and you'll find out how to trade Sprites. Who knows, you might get lucky and have someone gift you the Golden Zero Point Sprite!

We have more Sprite-related Fortnite guides you can find here:

  • How to Get The Burnt Peanut Sprite
  • How to Get the Zero Point Sprite
  • How to Get Gold Sprites
  • How to Get the Punk Sprite
  • How to Extract Sprites
  • How to Use the Portable Extractor
  • All Sprite Safari Quests
  • Mastery Mondays, Explained

Fortnite

Follow
Followed
Survival
Battle Royale
Shooter
Action
Adventure
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 84 /100 Critics Rec: 94%
Released
September 26, 2017
ESRB
T for Teen - Diverse Content: Discretion Advised, In-Game Purchases, Users Interact
Developer(s)
Epic Games
Publisher(s)
Epic Games
7 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Genre(s)
Survival, Battle Royale, Shooter, Action, Adventure
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Sprites are running amok in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3, and each of them has its own unique power and rarity, which is why you might be on a quest to hunt them all and add them to your collection! Luckily for you, we know where they are.

Below, you'll find a full Fortnite map showcasing all Sprite Chest locations , which you can use as a guide while venturing through the map to locate and open any active spawns to see if you get lucky enough to catch a rare Sprite, like the Zero Point, or even a Gold!

Sprite Chests Locations Map in Fortnite

Above, you can find the locations of every potential spawn of a Sprite Chest in Fortnite . It's worth noting that there won't be a Sprite Chest at every one of these spots in a single game, as Sprite Chests will spawn at random at the locations provided , and we may have missed a few considering the map is so large.

For hunting Sprite Chests, I'd recommend making sure you have Visualized Sounds on, as that will provide you with a direction on the HUD when near a Sprite Chest. I'd also recommend trying to hunt them in Zero Build, thanks to the Shockwave Grenade you can get every 30 seconds to navigate the map more easily.

All Sprites can be found in Sprite Chests, and we've got the odds and chances as percentage values for you to see right here.

If you're attempting to hunt Sprites in bot lobbies, it's worth noting that Fortnite added a hotfix where only Water, Fire, and Earth Sprites will appear in this mode.

Best Route to Take to Farm Sprite Chests in Fortnite

The link above will direct you to a guide showcasing our preferred route for finding Sprite Chests in a single match. This guide also covers the path in a video walkthrough (and a map) so you can see exactly where we go so you can avoid hostile players and focus purely on Sprites.

Instead of spending time farming Sprites, you can always try to trade with the community! Click the link provided, and you'll find out how to trade Sprites. Who knows, you might get lucky and have someone gift you the Golden Zero Point Sprite!

We have more Sprite-related Fortnite guides you can find here:

  • How to Get The Burnt Peanut Sprite
  • How to Get the Zero Point Sprite
  • How to Get Gold Sprites
  • How to Get the Punk Sprite
  • How to Extract Sprites
  • How to Use the Portable Extractor
  • All Sprite Safari Quests

item.title: The Only 2026 Open-World Games That Are 10/10, No Notes (So Far)
item.description:
Despite a seemingly quiet start to 2026 (at least, compared to what's coming in September and October), this year has actually been a huge success for the gaming industry. There have been very few misses and a ton of hits, from indie successes to triple-A bangers, and the year isn't even halfway done yet.

Much of that success has come in the open-world genre. Some games that weren't even expected to be fully open-world have stepped in and delivered some of the best entries the genre has seen in a while. Therefore, even though we've still got seven months' worth of games to go, let's take a look at the 2026 open-world games that are already 10/10 hits.

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games
Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.
Easy (5)Medium (7)Hard (10)

Your Order

Results

—
— —
More Games

Crimson Desert

An Explorer's Dream

Crimson Desert
Released
March 19, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

ESRB
Mature 17+ / Blood, Drug Reference, Intense Violence, Strong Language
Developer(s)
Pearl Abyss
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure, Open-World, Exploration
Powered by

It took a bit, but Crimson Desert has definitely earned itself a lot of praise following a rocky launch. With millions of units sold and a ton of positive word of mouth from players around the world, this open-world action RPG is winning over new players every day. The fact that it has been updated with new content multiple times since launch doesn't hurt, either.

Crimson Desert is not going to win any accolades for its narrative, and its combat system is very unusual and takes a while to get used to (although once you do, it feels fantastic). No, the major appeal of this game is just how incredible it feels to explore its world. There are secrets hidden everywhere, many of which have no quest marker or even landmarks to indicate their presence. It's a game that encourages its players to explore wherever and however they like, and it consistently rewards that exploration with incredible finds. This is the kind of game where people will still be uncovering secrets this time next year.

Forza Horizon 6

A Visit To The Mecca Of Car Culture

Forza Horizon 6
9 /10
Released
May 19, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
Everyone / Mild Lyrics, Users Interact, In-Game Purchases
Developer(s)
Playground Games
Genre(s)
Racing, Open-World
Powered by

Not a ton has changed between Forza Horizon 5 and Forza Horizon 6, but that's not a bad thing. Following up one of the greatest racing games of all time with another game just like it is hardly a knock against this sequel. The biggest change is its new setting, which finally takes the series to Japan to celebrate one of the most avid car cultures on the planet. And celebrate it does.

There is a smaller focus on off-road racing in FH6, mostly because there is less off-road terrain in the game's map, but that is replaced by a much more varied environment, from bustling cities to gorgeous rural vistas. The reward system has been tweaked to make winning currency feel more useful, as you won't unlock every car through Wheel Spins anymore. The rest is pretty much just more Forza Horizon, and that's all we really wanted anyway.

Cairn

Not Your Typical Open World

Cairn
Simulation
Adventure
Survival
Systems
Released
January 29, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
The Game Bakers
Genre(s)
Simulation, Adventure, Survival
Powered by

Cairn is not a standard open-world game. It's set entirely on a massive mountain, and while you are free to approach it however you like, you can't jump from the base right to the top. You'll have to tackle it methodically, like a real mountain climber, choosing your routes and planning well in advance so you don't get stuck or forced to retreat and find a new route.

So while it's not a typical open world in the exploration sense, you are still free to approach the climb however you see fit. Take any route you like, stop and camp whenever you want, and track down lost climbers as you go. There's still lots of variety in how you approach the game, and its singular focus is what makes it so special. You don't need to worry about side quests or filling out the map. You have one goal, reaching the mountain top, and how you get there is what makes your personal journey unique.

Esoteric Ebb

A Disco-Like Paying Homage To Terry Pratchett

Esoteric Ebb
CRPG
RPG
Isometric
Point-and-click
Adventure
Systems
Released
March 3, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Christoffer Bodegård
Genre(s)
CRPG, RPG, Isometric, Point-and-click, Adventure
Powered by

There has been a wave of " Disco -likes" lately, which borrow many of the iconic elements of Disco Elysium, and other than ZA/UM's follow-up, Zero Parades for Dead Spies (which is not open world), the best example of this trend is a weird little indie RPG called Esoteric Ebb. Despite reviewing well, this game still hasn't been discussed much, but that's bound to change because it's absolutely brilliant.

Instead of your inner thoughts being speaking characters, in Esoteric Ebb, it's your stat categories like Strength and Charisma that do the talking. It's a delightful twist, especially for DnD fans, and it's always funny to hear these stats bickering over a minor in-game decision. Unlike Disco Elysium, there is combat in Esoteric Ebb, although it's still defined by a dice roll. The whole thing has a distinct Terry Pratchett Discworld vibe to it that makes for the perfect humorous twist on a fantasy realm, and it delivers on all fronts. The narrative is great, the RPG systems are complex, and the voice acting is stellar. What's not to like?

Mina The Hollower

A Tribute To Retro Gaming That Outdoes Modern Classics

Mina the Hollower
Platformer
Action
Adventure
Systems
Released
May 29, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Everyone 10+ / Fantasy Violence
Developer(s)
Yacht Club Games
Genre(s)
Platformer, Action, Adventure
Powered by

Yacht Club's Shovel Knight redefined how indie games could interpret retro aesthetics and mechanics into a modern package, and it's something that we've seen countless games follow-up on since, from horror games like Crow Country to throwbacks like Ninja Gaiden Ragebound. Yacht Club is at it again, this time turning a lens on old-school The Legend of Zelda games and creating something truly outstanding.

Mina the Hollower doesn't show all its cards right away, and between its extremely understated visual style and its simple but intensely challenging combat, it will take a bit to get used to. Once you get the hang of it, though, this is a game that will take over your life. There are secrets everywhere, just waiting for you to spot the tiny clues giving them away. Boss fights are a standout here, with some boss sprites being truly spectacular. The combat is pitch-perfect, with dozens of options for customization, and it even allows you to tweak the difficulty in over a dozen different ways if you find the game too challenging. It's almost a certainty that we'll be talking about Mina the Hollower during 2026's GOTY deliberations.

Pokemon Pokopia

A Stupidly Addictive Sandbox, And The Best Pokemon Game In Years

Pokemon Pokopia
9 /10
Released
March 5, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

ESRB
Everyone / Users Interact, In-Game Purchases
Developer(s)
Koei Tecmo, Game Freak
Genre(s)
Life Simulation, Simulation
Powered by

Drawing heavily from games like Animal Crossing: New Horizons and Stardew Valley, Pokemon Pokopia snuck up on everybody. The Pokemon games have been in a weird spot lately, with a lot of the recent entries feeling like half-baked ideas rather than true next-gen experiences. That's why it surprised everyone when Pokemon Pokopia wasn't just good, but it was one of the best Pokemon games in recent memory.

Subscribe to our newsletter for open-world gaming picks

Unlock deeper open-world gaming insights - subscribe to our newsletter for curated reviews, deep dives on standout titles and hidden gems, and coverage that helps you spot the year's must-play open-world experiences and wider gaming trends.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

If you're familiar with sandbox games, then you know more or less what you'll get here, but it's the Pokemon twist that makes it so special. The game is set in a "post-apocalyptic" landscape where all the humans have vanished. You play as a Ditto who shoulders the task of restoring the world. It's a special kind of emotion when you realize that all the Pokemon who were left behind truly cannot help themselves without a trainer around, and what it means to them when you come to the rescue. Pokopia is a faster-paced sandbox than Animal Crossing, and provides more space for activities, which keeps things moving and makes it absurdly addictive. Best of all, the game is absolutely loaded with content (with more on the way), guaranteeing that you'll lose dozens of hours in its surprisingly rich world.
item.content:encoded:
Despite a seemingly quiet start to 2026 (at least, compared to what's coming in September and October), this year has actually been a huge success for the gaming industry. There have been very few misses and a ton of hits, from indie successes to triple-A bangers, and the year isn't even halfway done yet.

Much of that success has come in the open-world genre. Some games that weren't even expected to be fully open-world have stepped in and delivered some of the best entries the genre has seen in a while. Therefore, even though we've still got seven months' worth of games to go, let's take a look at the 2026 open-world games that are already 10/10 hits.

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games

Crimson Desert

An Explorer's Dream

Crimson Desert
Released
March 19, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

ESRB
Mature 17+ / Blood, Drug Reference, Intense Violence, Strong Language
Developer(s)
Pearl Abyss
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure, Open-World, Exploration
Powered by

It took a bit, but Crimson Desert has definitely earned itself a lot of praise following a rocky launch. With millions of units sold and a ton of positive word of mouth from players around the world, this open-world action RPG is winning over new players every day. The fact that it has been updated with new content multiple times since launch doesn't hurt, either.

Crimson Desert is not going to win any accolades for its narrative, and its combat system is very unusual and takes a while to get used to (although once you do, it feels fantastic). No, the major appeal of this game is just how incredible it feels to explore its world. There are secrets hidden everywhere, many of which have no quest marker or even landmarks to indicate their presence. It's a game that encourages its players to explore wherever and however they like, and it consistently rewards that exploration with incredible finds. This is the kind of game where people will still be uncovering secrets this time next year.

Forza Horizon 6

A Visit To The Mecca Of Car Culture

Forza Horizon 6
9 /10
Released
May 19, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
Everyone / Mild Lyrics, Users Interact, In-Game Purchases
Developer(s)
Playground Games
Genre(s)
Racing, Open-World
Powered by

Not a ton has changed between Forza Horizon 5 and Forza Horizon 6, but that's not a bad thing. Following up one of the greatest racing games of all time with another game just like it is hardly a knock against this sequel. The biggest change is its new setting, which finally takes the series to Japan to celebrate one of the most avid car cultures on the planet. And celebrate it does.

There is a smaller focus on off-road racing in FH6, mostly because there is less off-road terrain in the game's map, but that is replaced by a much more varied environment, from bustling cities to gorgeous rural vistas. The reward system has been tweaked to make winning currency feel more useful, as you won't unlock every car through Wheel Spins anymore. The rest is pretty much just more Forza Horizon, and that's all we really wanted anyway.

Cairn

Not Your Typical Open World

Cairn
Simulation
Adventure
Survival
Systems
Released
January 29, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
The Game Bakers
Genre(s)
Simulation, Adventure, Survival
Powered by

Cairn is not a standard open-world game. It's set entirely on a massive mountain, and while you are free to approach it however you like, you can't jump from the base right to the top. You'll have to tackle it methodically, like a real mountain climber, choosing your routes and planning well in advance so you don't get stuck or forced to retreat and find a new route.

So while it's not a typical open world in the exploration sense, you are still free to approach the climb however you see fit. Take any route you like, stop and camp whenever you want, and track down lost climbers as you go. There's still lots of variety in how you approach the game, and its singular focus is what makes it so special. You don't need to worry about side quests or filling out the map. You have one goal, reaching the mountain top, and how you get there is what makes your personal journey unique.

Esoteric Ebb

A Disco-Like Paying Homage To Terry Pratchett

Esoteric Ebb
CRPG

Isometric
Point-and-click
Adventure

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Christoffer Bodegård
Genre(s)
CRPG, RPG, Isometric, Point-and-click, Adventure
Powered by

There has been a wave of " Disco -likes" lately, which borrow many of the iconic elements of Disco Elysium, and other than ZA/UM's follow-up, Zero Parades for Dead Spies (which is not open world), the best example of this trend is a weird little indie RPG called Esoteric Ebb. Despite reviewing well, this game still hasn't been discussed much, but that's bound to change because it's absolutely brilliant.

Instead of your inner thoughts being speaking characters, in Esoteric Ebb, it's your stat categories like Strength and Charisma that do the talking. It's a delightful twist, especially for DnD fans, and it's always funny to hear these stats bickering over a minor in-game decision. The whole thing has a distinct Terry Pratchett Discworld vibe to it that makes for the perfect humorous twist on a fantasy realm, and it delivers on all fronts. The narrative is great, the RPG systems are complex, and the voice acting is stellar. What's not to like?

Mina The Hollower

A Tribute To Retro Gaming That Outdoes Modern Classics

Mina the Hollower
Platformer

Adventure
Systems

May 29, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Everyone 10+ / Fantasy Violence
Developer(s)
Yacht Club Games
Genre(s)
Platformer, Action, Adventure
Powered by

Yacht Club's Shovel Knight redefined how indie games could interpret retro aesthetics and mechanics into a modern package, and it's something that we've seen countless games follow-up on since, from horror games like Crow Country to throwbacks like Ninja Gaiden Ragebound. Yacht Club is at it again, this time turning a lens on old-school The Legend of Zelda games and creating something truly outstanding.

Mina the Hollower doesn't show all its cards right away, and between its extremely understated visual style and its simple but intensely challenging combat, it will take a bit to get used to. Once you get the hang of it, though, this is a game that will take over your life. There are secrets everywhere, just waiting for you to spot the tiny clues giving them away. Boss fights are a standout here, with some boss sprites being truly spectacular. The combat is pitch-perfect, with dozens of options for customization, and it even allows you to tweak the difficulty in over a dozen different ways if you find the game too challenging. It's almost a certainty that we'll be talking about Mina the Hollower during 2026's GOTY deliberations.

Pokemon Pokopia

A Stupidly Addictive Sandbox, And The Best Pokemon Game In Years

Pokemon Pokopia
9 /10
Released
March 5, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

ESRB
Everyone / Users Interact, In-Game Purchases
Developer(s)
Koei Tecmo, Game Freak
Genre(s)
Life Simulation, Simulation
Powered by

Drawing heavily from games like Animal Crossing: New Horizons and Stardew Valley, Pokemon Pokopia snuck up on everybody. The Pokemon games have been in a weird spot lately, with a lot of the recent entries feeling like half-baked ideas rather than true next-gen experiences. That's why it surprised everyone when Pokemon Pokopia wasn't just good, but it was one of the best Pokemon games in recent memory.

Subscribe to our newsletter for open-world gaming picks

Unlock deeper open-world gaming insights - subscribe to our newsletter for curated reviews, deep dives on standout titles and hidden gems, and coverage that helps you spot the year's must-play open-world experiences and wider gaming trends.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

If you're familiar with sandbox games, then you know more or less what you'll get here, but it's the Pokemon twist that makes it so special. The game is set in a "post-apocalyptic" landscape where all the humans have vanished. You play as a Ditto who shoulders the task of restoring the world. It's a special kind of emotion when you realize that all the Pokemon who were left behind truly cannot help themselves without a trainer around, and what it means to them when you come to the rescue. Pokopia is a faster-paced sandbox than Animal Crossing, and provides more space for activities, which keeps things moving and makes it absurdly addictive. Best of all, the game is absolutely loaded with content (with more on the way), guaranteeing that you'll lose dozens of hours in its surprisingly rich world.

item.title: Blasphemous 2: How to Remove the Purple Mist
item.description:
After reaching the Castle of the Mis t and starting the DLC, players will be able to explore the multiple new areas the free expansion has to offer. During exploration, you will come across certain rooms covered in a Purple Mist where blue needles constantly fly toward you. If five needles gather around your character, they will deal damage and respawn you a short distance away, similar to how spiked traps function.

While these hazards are usually manageable, they can sometimes make entire rooms inaccessible or prevent progression. Thankfully, there is an easy way to remove the mist permanently in Blasphemous 2 .

How to Clear the Purple Mist in Blasphemous 2

The first step toward removing the Purple Mist is obtaining the new DLC weapon, Embrujo . This weapon can be found to the west of the Prie Dieu in Geometry of Knowledge . Once acquired, you'll gain the ability to use R1/RB on your controller near specific hooks, allowing you to grapple through many of the areas introduced in the DLC.

With Embrujo in your possession, make your way to the Prie Dieu located roughly in the middle of Stone Entrails .

From here, follow the route below:

  • From the shrine, head right, climb down the ladder, and enter the room on the right.
  • Drop down and enter the room on your left. Ignore the winged creatures that descend from the ceiling and continue down the ladder.
  • You'll spot a mini-boss version of the first DLC boss in this area. Fortunately, you can completely ignore the encounter and continue onward.
  • In the middle of the room is another ladder leading down into an area covered by Purple Mist. Dash through the gap on the left and climb down the second ladder.
  • Drop down on the left side, descend the next ladder, then quickly dash left and use your grapple ability twice with R1/RB.
  • Enter the room on your left. Inside, you'll find numerous coffins along with a figure resting inside an open coffin.
  • Attack the figure to trigger a dialogue sequence.

After the conversation ends, the Purple Mist throughout the DLC will disappear permanently, allowing you to freely access previously blocked rooms and continue exploring the rest of The Third Sin expansion without worrying about the blue needle mechanic.

More Blasphemous 2 Guides

If you need help with some of the Blasphemous 2 guides, make sure to check these out:

  • All Empty Receptacle Locations
  • All Wax Seed Locations
  • How to Get Both Endings

Blasphemous 2

Follow
Followed
Soulslike
Metroidvania
Platformer
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 84 /100 Critics Rec: 93%
Released
August 24, 2023
ESRB
M For Mature 17+ // Blood and Gore, Partial Nudity, Violence
Developer(s)
The Game Kitchen
Publisher(s)
Team17

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
FX Wiki Wiki

Genre(s)
Soulslike, Metroidvania, Platformer
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
After reaching the Castle of the Mis t and starting the DLC, players will be able to explore the multiple new areas the free expansion has to offer. During exploration, you will come across certain rooms covered in a Purple Mist where blue needles constantly fly toward you. If five needles gather around your character, they will deal damage and respawn you a short distance away, similar to how spiked traps function.

While these hazards are usually manageable, they can sometimes make entire rooms inaccessible or prevent progression. Thankfully, there is an easy way to remove the mist permanently in Blasphemous 2 .

How to Clear the Purple Mist in Blasphemous 2

The first step toward removing the Purple Mist is obtaining the new DLC weapon, Embrujo . This weapon can be found to the west of the Prie Dieu in Geometry of Knowledge . Once acquired, you'll gain the ability to use R1/RB on your controller near specific hooks, allowing you to grapple through many of the areas introduced in the DLC.

With Embrujo in your possession, make your way to the Prie Dieu located roughly in the middle of Stone Entrails .

From here, follow the route below:

  • From the shrine, head right, climb down the ladder, and enter the room on the right.
  • Drop down and enter the room on your left. Ignore the winged creatures that descend from the ceiling and continue down the ladder.
  • You'll spot a mini-boss version of the first DLC boss in this area. Fortunately, you can completely ignore the encounter and continue onward.
  • In the middle of the room is another ladder leading down into an area covered by Purple Mist. Dash through the gap on the left and climb down the second ladder.
  • Drop down on the left side, descend the next ladder, then quickly dash left and use your grapple ability twice with R1/RB.
  • Enter the room on your left. Inside, you'll find numerous coffins along with a figure resting inside an open coffin.
  • Attack the figure to trigger a dialogue sequence.

After the conversation ends, the Purple Mist throughout the DLC will disappear permanently, allowing you to freely access previously blocked rooms and continue exploring the rest of The Third Sin expansion without worrying about the blue needle mechanic.

More Blasphemous 2 Guides

If you need help with some of the Blasphemous 2 guides, make sure to check these out:

  • All Empty Receptacle Locations
  • All Wax Seed Locations
  • How to Get Both Endings

Blasphemous 2

Systems

Developer(s)

Publisher(s)
Team17

WHERE TO PLAY

Genre(s)

Powered by

item.title: RECLONE Codes (June 2026)
item.description:
RECLONE is a fun sci-fi stealth horror game on Roblox where you explore a creepy abandoned research ship in the void of space. Your main goal is to sneak through dark hallways and stay away from the scary Hunter who is always looking for you.

Codes let you grab free rewards quickly so you can enjoy more of the adventure without waiting too long. Here are the latest working codes for RECLONE that you can use right now for free Tokens!

All RECLONE Codes

Working RECLONE Codes

Code

Reward(s)

BREACH

50 Tokens

Expired RECLONE Codes

There are no expired codes at the moment. All the codes we know about are still working fine.

The Tokens you receive can be used to buy better gear and cool skins for your items. This makes your character look unique and can even help you survive longer against the Hunter.

When you redeem working promo codes, you can try out new looks and upgrades without spending a lot of time collecting resources first!

How to Redeem RECLONE Codes

  • Open RECLONE on Roblox and join the game.
  • Look for a button that says “Shop.”
  • Click the Codes button to open the code box.
  • Type the code exactly as it appears, with no extra spaces.
  • Press the redeem button and watch your rewards appear in your account.

How to Get More RECLONE Codes

  • The developers often share new codes first on their official Discord server.
  • They also post updates in the official Firebolt Games group on Roblox .

We update this article regularly whenever fresh working codes for this Roblox game are released. The easiest way to keep track of the latest codes is to check back here from time to time so you never miss a reward.

Roblox
Released
September 1, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Teen / Diverse Content: Discretion Advised, In-Game Purchases (Includes Random Items), Users Interact
Genre(s)
Game Creation System
Platform(s)
PlayStation 4, Xbox One, PC, Android, iOS, Meta Quest 2, Meta Quest Pro
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
RECLONE is a fun sci-fi stealth horror game on Roblox where you explore a creepy abandoned research ship in the void of space. Your main goal is to sneak through dark hallways and stay away from the scary Hunter who is always looking for you.

Codes let you grab free rewards quickly so you can enjoy more of the adventure without waiting too long. Here are the latest working codes for RECLONE that you can use right now for free Tokens!

All RECLONE Codes

Working RECLONE Codes

Code

Reward(s)

BREACH

50 Tokens

Expired RECLONE Codes

There are no expired codes at the moment. All the codes we know about are still working fine.

The Tokens you receive can be used to buy better gear and cool skins for your items. This makes your character look unique and can even help you survive longer against the Hunter.

When you redeem working promo codes, you can try out new looks and upgrades without spending a lot of time collecting resources first!

How to Redeem RECLONE Codes

  • Open RECLONE on Roblox and join the game.
  • Look for a button that says “Shop.”
  • Click the Codes button to open the code box.
  • Type the code exactly as it appears, with no extra spaces.
  • Press the redeem button and watch your rewards appear in your account.

How to Get More RECLONE Codes

  • The developers often share new codes first on their official Discord server.
  • They also post updates in the official Firebolt Games group on Roblox .

We update this article regularly whenever fresh working codes for this Roblox game are released. The easiest way to keep track of the latest codes is to check back here from time to time so you never miss a reward.

Roblox
Released
September 1, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Teen / Diverse Content: Discretion Advised, In-Game Purchases (Includes Random Items), Users Interact
Genre(s)
Game Creation System
Platform(s)
PlayStation 4, Xbox One, PC, Android, iOS, Meta Quest 2, Meta Quest Pro
Powered by

item.title: Incredible PC Games That Will Run on a Potato Laptop
item.description:
As everything gets more expensive, low-end PC gaming grows ever more attractive and, frankly, necessary. You don't need to have an RTX 4090 with water cooling and the latest processor to not only enjoy yourself, but also to be spoiled with choices. Steam caters to pretty much every type of gamer, and that includes thousands of games that can be run on old laptops or PCs with a graphics card more than a decade old. Sure, they might not be able to run Crimson Desert or Forza Horizon 6 , but they still might be able to run one of 2026's highest-rated games so far.

As great as Valve's platform is, Steam also has a serious slop issue that can make it difficult to pinpoint the accessible games worth playing. Well, let's try to simplify that process as much as possible with a list of recommendations across a spectrum of low-end tiers.

Last Updated on June 8, 2026 by Mark Sammut:

  • Added Mina the Hallower
  • Added Bongo Cat
  • Rewrote intro

Systemrequirementslab is a useful resource to check whether your computer can run a game.

New Low-Spec Games

Mina the Hollower

Mina the Hollower
Platformer
Action
Adventure
Systems
Released
May 29, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Yacht Club Games
Genre(s)
Platformer, Action, Adventure
Platform(s)
PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S, Nintendo Switch 2, Nintendo Switch, PC
Powered by
  • Potato Tier: Tier 1
  • The Secret 2026 Performance Fix: Nothing to note for now.

One of the bigger releases of 2026, Mina the Hollower comes from the team behind Shovel Knight , a connection that set expectations absurdly high. For the most part, Yacht Club Games more than managed to live up to its own legacy, crafting a new indie gem that very much sets itself apart from the previous franchise. Very much a throwback to the days when 8-bit games were considered revolutionary, Mina the Hollower combines classic Zelda with modern controls and intense combat that requires patience and mastery. If you have a particular fondness for Game Boy Color-era RPGs, this game should hit you right in the nostalgia.

Obviously, games with these types of visuals cater to low-end systems by design, so it shouldn't come as a surprise that Mina the Hollower runs on even Intel HD 5000 graphics. Steam is absolutely flooded with these types of releases, to the point that they no longer feel all that special. However, Mina the Hollower is special, and it is one of the most well-rounded games released in recent months.

Minimum System Requirements

Operating System

Windows 8 (earliest)

Processor

Intel Core 4th generation (Haswell, 2013) / AMD Bulldozer (2011)

RAM

4 GB

Graphics Card

Intel HD 5000 (2013) / Radeon HD 7000 (2012) / Nvidia 900 Series (Maxwell, 2014)

Storage

860 MB

Runs On Integrated Graphics

Vampire Crawlers: The Turbo Wildcard from Vampire Survivors

A Masterpiece That Runs On Most Systems

Vampire Crawlers: The Turbo Wildcard from Vampire Survivors
Roguelike
Deckbuilding
Dungeon Crawler
Systems
Released
April 21, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Poncle
Genre(s)
Roguelike, Deckbuilding, Dungeon Crawler
Platform(s)
PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S, Nintendo Switch 2, Nintendo Switch, PC
Powered by
  • Potato Tier: Tier 1
  • The Secret 2026 Performance Fix: Due to being new, the game has bugs and issues that are still being ironed out. Keep an eye on Steam's Discussions page, as that is where fixes will be first posted.

Well, yeah, a Vampire Survivors spin-off runs on just about everything, as it would be against the brand if it didn't. More importantly, Vampire Crawlers: The Turbo Wildcard from Vampire Survivors is just as brilliant as its mother game, entering and establishing itself in a pretty oversaturated market. Steam is packed to the brim with roguelite deckbuilders, most of which are not demanding and pretty darn addictive. Even with the Vampire Survivors connection, Vampire Crawlers needed to make an instant impression to drag people away from Balatro or Slay the Spire 2 .

The most impressive thing is just how well the spin-off translates the appeal of the original game into this format. Deck-builders tend to be on the slower side, but Vampire Crawlers is surprisingly kinetic, filling each battle with explosive special effects that make every success incredibly satisfying. Sensory overload is the name of the game, and it is food for the brain.

Minimum System Requirements

Operating System

Windows 10

Processor

x64 architecture with SSE2

RAM

4 GB

Graphics Card

DX11, DX12

Storage

2 GB

Bongo Cat

A Little Friend On Your Screen

Bongo Cat
Comedy
Point-and-click
2D
Indie
Systems
Released
March 5, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Irox Games
Genre(s)
Comedy, Point-and-click, 2D, Indie
Platform(s)
PC
Powered by
  • Potato Tier: Tier 1
  • The Secret 2026 Performance Fix: Nothing.

OK, this one is a bit of a cheat, as Bongo Cat isn't exactly a "game." However, it is sold as one on Steam, and you can technically play it for longer than pretty much any other release. Basically, Bongo Cat is a desktop/laptop companion that counts every time you type a letter, granting rewards when milestones are reached. A very simple concept executed perfectly, this game can serve as extra encouragement when you find yourself struggling to find motivation to work on a project. Just let Bongo Cat cheer you along. Also, the game's free.

Minimum System Requirements

Operating System

Windows 10/11

Processor

Intel i5 5200 or AMD Ryzen 5 5600X

RAM

1 GB

Graphics Card

Any

Storage

100 MB

Esoteric Ebb

Disco Elysium + Dungeons & Dragons

  • Potato Tier: Tier 3
  • The Secret 2026 Performance Fix: Nothing right now due to its recency. This is a narrative-driven game, so while performance on an Iris Xe might not be optimal, it should still be playable.

While Steam's minimum specs advise a GTX 1050, Esoteric Ebb should work well enough for integrated graphics, as long as we are talking about relatively high-end stuff like the Iris Xe. A spiritual successor to Disco Elysium , this 2026 release adds a touch of Dungeons and Dragons via mechanics like saving throws, stats, and spell slots, and your decisions can very much ruin the "campaign." While you could end up becoming a superstar clerk who is put on a pedestal, you could also make one baffling choice after another, dooming the city and yourself.

Esoteric Ebb is a detective mystery similar to Disco Elysium , although its fantasy setting gives it a widely different feel and tone. It isn't as politically driven as that masterpiece, but it delivers great writing, unpredictable developments, and open-ended quests. The story also comes with a ticking clock angle, as the mystery needs to be tackled and solved within the 5 days left before an election. Start over.

Minimum System Requirements

Operating System

Windows 10

Processor

Intel i5-2500K; AMD FX-8350

RAM

8 GB

Graphics Card

NVIDIA GTZX 1050; AMD RX 560

Storage

8 GB

Hollow Knight: Silksong

The Holy Grail of Low-End PC Gaming

  • Potato Tier: Tier 2
  • The Secret 2026 Performance Fix: Set resolution to 720p; the art style hides the downscaling perfectly.

The 7-year itch is gone – Silksong is finally out. More importantly, Hollow Knight 's long-awaited sequel looks gorgeous and runs beautifully, which should not come as a surprise considering its predecessor's quality. Despite nearly a decade passing since HK 's debut, and Team Cherry nowadays being one of the most well-known developers ever, Silksong is still very much an indie Metroidvania that is intended to be as accessible as possible, at least when it comes to specs. The game is easy to run, as all you need is a 2012 i3 CPU and a 2011 GPU to meet the minimum requirements. If you have a moderately new system, you will probably be able to run Silksong at its recommended specs.

Hype can be both a good and bad thing, and few games have had as much hype as Silksong . While garnering its share of criticisms, which is to be expected of any project, the sequel has mostly garnered a standing ovation for its challenging but fun boss fights, good gameplay, amount of content, and world design. Silksong is also notoriously difficult, especially for people who might not have dropped a million hours in Hollow Knight . Even the latter's veterans will need to unlearn a few things if they want to beat Team Cherry's latest masterpiece.

Minimum System Requirements

Operating System

Windows 10

Processor

Intel Core i3-3240; AMD FX-4300

RAM

4 GB

Graphics Card

GeForce GTX 560 Ti; Radeon HD 7750

Storage

8 GB

Hades

The Best Triple-I Game Ever

  • Potato Tier: Tier 1
  • The Secret 2026 Performance Fix: Hades will run at a stable 60 FPS as long as you stay at 720p or 1080p. Since the art is hand-painted 2D/2.5D, it looks fine on low settings.

Supergiant's Hades is comfortably among the most popular roguelites on the market, and it serves as a good entry point for newcomers to the genre. The narrative follows Zagreus, the eponymous god's son, who wants to leave the Underworld and make his way to Olympus. However, his father rejects this plan, and he will not make this journey easy for the young prince. As a roguelite, Hades ' loop revolves around replaying the same stages; however, unlike many similar games, defeat does not result in a complete reset. Instead, Zagreus' story develops naturally following a failure, so there is a constant sense of progression during the first 20 hours or so.

Boasting a gorgeous art style and fast-paced hack and slash combat, Hades knocks its presentation out of the park. Although an indie release, Supergiant's title is so polished and visually striking that it outshines most AAA projects; however, unlike the latest gaming blockbusters, Hades does not require a high-end PC. In fact, the game is really not demanding.

Minimum System Requirements

Operating System

Windows 7 SP1

Processor

Dual Core 2.4 GHz

RAM

4 GB

Graphics Card

1 GB memory (dedicated VRAM or shared RAM), DirectX 10+

Storage

15 GB

Ultrakill

The Best Shooter of the 2020s is Optimized To Perfection

  • Potato Tier: Tier 1
  • The Secret 2026 Performance Fix: Enable PSX Mode in settings; it’s stylized and improves FPS.

Steam is stuffed with modern boomer shooters, most of which use old-school visuals that make them easily workable on most decent systems. As fun as most of these games are, they do have a habit of blending, creating an undecipherable blob of shotguns, pixilated body parts, and gore. Despite adhering to this trend, Ultrakill succeeds in standing out from the crowd, providing an experience that is both unique and representative of the FPS genre at its best.

The goal of the game is not just to kill but to do it in style, to the point of containing a point system reminiscent of Devil May Cry . In fact, Ultrakill combines the thrill of fast-paced shooters with the player-based reward system of character action games, a completely harmonious marriage. As long as somebody doesn't mind high difficulty and early access releases, they should add Ultrakill to their library.

Minimum System Requirements

Operating System

Windows 10 (64-bit)

Processor

2.4GHz (Min)

RAM

4 GB

Graphics Card

GTX 560 or equivalent

Storage

3 GB
item.content:encoded:
As everything gets more expensive, low-end PC gaming grows ever more attractive and, frankly, necessary. You don't need to have an RTX 4090 with water cooling and the latest processor to not only enjoy yourself, but also to be spoiled with choices. Steam caters to pretty much every type of gamer, and that includes thousands of games that can be run on old laptops or PCs with a graphics card more than a decade old. Sure, they might not be able to run Crimson Desert or Forza Horizon 6 , but they still might be able to run one of 2026's highest-rated games so far.

As great as Valve's platform is, Steam also has a serious slop issue that can make it difficult to pinpoint the accessible games worth playing. Well, let's try to simplify that process as much as possible with a list of recommendations across a spectrum of low-end tiers.

For the most part, Yacht Club Games more than managed to live up to its own legacy, crafting a new indie gem that very much sets itself apart from the previous franchise. Steam is absolutely flooded with these types of releases, to the point that they no longer feel all that special. The 7-year itch is gone – Silksong is finally out. More importantly, Hollow Knight 's long-awaited sequel looks gorgeous and runs beautifully, which should not come as a surprise considering its predecessor's quality. Despite nearly a decade passing since HK 's debut, and Team Cherry nowadays being one of the most well-known developers ever, Silksong is still very much an indie Metroidvania that is intended to be as accessible as possible, at least when it comes to specs. The game is easy to run, as all you need is a 2012 i3 CPU and a 2011 GPU to meet the minimum requirements. If you have a moderately new system, you will probably be able to run Silksong at its recommended specs.

Hype can be both a good and bad thing, and few games have had as much hype as Silksong . While garnering its share of criticisms, which is to be expected of any project, the sequel has mostly garnered a standing ovation for its challenging but fun boss fights, good gameplay, amount of content, and world design. Windows 10

RAM

Graphics Card

GeForce GTX 560 Ti; Radeon HD 7750

  • The Secret 2026 Performance Fix: Hades will run at a stable 60 FPS as long as you stay at 720p or 1080p. Since the art is hand-painted 2D/2.5D, it looks fine on low settings.

Supergiant's Hades is comfortably among the most popular roguelites on the market, and it serves as a good entry point for newcomers to the genre. The narrative follows Zagreus, the eponymous god's son, who wants to leave the Underworld and make his way to Olympus. However, his father rejects this plan, and he will not make this journey easy for the young prince. As a roguelite, Hades ' loop revolves around replaying the same stages; however, unlike many similar games, defeat does not result in a complete reset. Instead, Zagreus' story develops naturally following a failure, so there is a constant sense of progression during the first 20 hours or so.

Boasting a gorgeous art style and fast-paced hack and slash combat, Hades knocks its presentation out of the park. Although an indie release, Supergiant's title is so polished and visually striking that it outshines most AAA projects; however, unlike the latest gaming blockbusters, Hades does not require a high-end PC. The Best Shooter of the 2020s is Optimized To Perfection

  • Potato Tier: Tier 1
  • The Secret 2026 Performance Fix: Enable PSX Mode in settings; it’s stylized and improves FPS.

Steam is stuffed with modern boomer shooters, most of which use old-school visuals that make them easily workable on most decent systems. As fun as most of these games are, they do have a habit of blending, creating an undecipherable blob of shotguns, pixilated body parts, and gore. Despite adhering to this trend, Ultrakill succeeds in standing out from the crowd, providing an experience that is both unique and representative of the FPS genre at its best.

The goal of the game is not just to kill but to do it in style, to the point of containing a point system reminiscent of Devil May Cry . In fact, Ultrakill combines the thrill of fast-paced shooters with the player-based reward system of character action games, a completely harmonious marriage.
